title: kentrochrosia carolinensis
intro: Kentrochrosia carolinensis, also known as the Flame of the Woods, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of the Western Ghats in India. It is known for its vibrant orange-red flowers and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and acting as an antihistamine. Herbal preparations of Kentrochrosia carolinensis include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
